original_abstract: This community service initiative addresses the need for economic independence and entrepreneurial character building among students at Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan. Utilizing an Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) framework, the program leverages existing institutional resources—including food service units, bottled water production, and aquaculture—as practical laboratories for learning. The project engaged 10th and 11th-grade students through a structured methodology involving theoretical training in Sharia-compliant mu’amalah (economic transactions) and hands-on operational experience within the pesantren’s business units. Evaluation through pre-test and post-test assessments revealed a 37% improvement in student understanding, demonstrating that participants successfully learned to identify market opportunities and navigate business challenges within an Islamic ethical framework. By reframing the pesantren’s physical and human assets as drivers for growth, this initiative not only equipped students with vocational skills but also fostered a paradigm shift toward self-sufficiency. The results suggest that integrating religious education with practical entrepreneurship can effectively prepare students for post-pesantren life, providing a sustainable model for community-based economic empowerment in similar educational settings.
humanized_abstract: Titel (de) Titel (en) Students at Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan Gained Practical Knowledge and Confidence in Becoming Entrepreneurs Through The Community Enterprise Project based on ABCD Theory. Photo: Muhammad Fadhil The Program aimed at enhancing the economic capabilities of students in Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan (PONAB) and building their entrepreneurship skills. This program utilizes an Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) theory which is a strategy and process that aims to empower local communities by analysing and maximizing their potentials, as well as using all the available assets within the community. The 10th and 11th-grade students of PONAB went through a series of training and methods where they first received theoretical lessons about Shariah mu’amalah (Islamic economic transactions) and then went on to learn the operation of various food stalls, a bottled water refilling station and an aquaculture business owned by PONAB. The increase in the students’ knowledge about Shariah mu’amalah after taking part in this entrepreneurship program reached 37% when compared to their scores before the pre-test and post-test that they went through. This proved that the students were able to recognize the opportunity of the market and increase their capabilities in dealing with business problems that follow the principles of Shariah Mu’amalah. By identifying the Pondok Pesantren’s physical and human resources as the opportunities and assets to be developed for increasing the students’ economy competence and empowering them to become independent, the PONAB entrepreneurship program shows the successful implementation of a pragmatic entrepreneurship approach which can be further developed as one of the approaches to increase the competitiveness of students in terms of employment and also as an alternative model to empower the economy of madrasa students in general.
